title: Google, Meta facing 'existential threat' after losing landmark addiction case | The World | ABC News
description: A Los Angeles jury has found Google and Meta liable for damages in a landmark social media addiction lawsuit that could influence thousands of cases. The plaintiff in the case, a 20-year-old woman, accused the tech companies of causing harm by deliberately designing addictive platforms and was awarded $4.3 million in damages. Jacob Ward, veteran tech journalist and host of the Rip Current podcast told The World's Girish Sawlani, the giant tech firms could be facing billions of dollars in future liabilities. title: After Iran and Venezuela, is Cuba next for Trump? | ABC News Daily podcast
description: Donald Trump is in the midst of a messy war with Iran, but at the same time his sights are set on Cuba.    The US leader says he’ll be having the “honour of taking Cuba”, which has been crippled by an oil embargo president Trump imposed earlier this year.    Today, Lillian Guerra, professor of Cuban history at the University of Florida on Trump’s next move.    Featured:    Lillian Guerra, professor of Cuban and Caribbean history at the University of Florida

title: Iran wants to 'horizontally escalate' war at great cost to Trump: Turnbull | ABC NEWS
description: Malcolm Turnbull tells News Breakfast that Australian government made the right decision in not getting involved in the Iran war given Israel and the US started the conflict without consultation. Iran's response has been to increase the cost of the war for Trump by attacking Gulf nations. Turnbull says the war is going to have massive ramifications for Trump domestically and within his own party. title: Iran rejects US ceasefire plan but doesn't close door to negotiations | ABC NEWS
description: Iran has rejected Washington’s 15-point ceasefire plan but has not closed the door entirely on renewed peace talks. The US demands included Iran dismantling its nuclear program, diminishing its ballistic missile capabilities, handing over uranium, cutting funding to proxy military groups the opening of the Strait of Hormuz. title: Severe TC Narelle sitting off Exmouth as Category 4 storm | ABC NEWS
description: Severe TC Narelle will impact the North West Cape region of WA today as a category 4 storm. Senior Meteorologist at the BOM says destructive winds are the main concern, with the system bringing winds of up to 260km/hr winds near the centre of the cyclone. The large warning area extends as far south as Jurien Bay. title: 'Monster cyclone' Narelle to make landfall on WA coast | ABC NEWS
description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is continuing to track along the West Australian coast.  The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting the west Pilbara coast.  The Bureau of Meteorology is warning that severe impacts will be felt along the Gascoyne coast through this morning. title: Tonga’s Prime Minister speaks on debt, drugs, and democracy | The Pacific | ABC News
description: Tonga’s Prime Minister Lord Fakafanua has reached his first 100 days in office stepping into a role defined by mounting challenges, from debt to China to a drugs crisis and a struggling national airline. title: German defence minister says Iran war started without clear objectives or exit strategy | ABC NEWS
description: The German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius has been visiting Australia as part of a regional tour to reinforce ties in the Asia Pacific.
